goal: Plan a family event on Saturday, June 12, 2026.
decisions: Decide to assign a fair, role-based setup and cleanup roster.
Nominate a toastmaster to keep things on time.
Appoint a playlist manager to curate and manage a multi-generational playlist for the event.
Explore hybrid option: hire a solo acoustic musician for the welcome/meal/toast portion.
Adopt vanilla with berries as the main cake flavor for the dessert, with a possible smaller adventurous cake or cupcakes as a secondary option.
Designate a Media Wrangler to manage photo/video backups during the event.
Create a shared cloud album for guest photo sharing and test uploads before the event.
Implement a two-backup rule: two copies on different media and one off-site/cloud; perform backups during the event.
Enforce toast length: 60–90 seconds per speaker and appoint the MC to manage timing and cutoffs.
Kid activity rotation plan: implement 2–3 stations for children with 1 supervising adult/older teen per 2–3 stations, rotating every 20–30 minutes.
entities: date: Saturday, June 12, 2026
headcount: 42
budget: $2,500
alcohol: none
vibe: casual, relaxed
decor: open to DIY decorations if practical
volunteers: several cousins offered to help with setup and cleanup
schedule: light, with a short welcome, a couple of brief toasts, and low-key all-ages group activities
playlist: multi-generational playlist planned (sample list of 30 songs provided)
music: curated playlist with optional solo acoustic musician for the welcome/meal/toast
cake flavor: vanilla with berries
backup protocol: two backups on two different media and one off-site/cloud; test uploads via cloud album
centerpieces: centerpieces should be low (8–12 inches) for visibility and wind stability
centerpieces: use low-weight, wind-stable materials such as mason jars with gravel and compact flower clusters
centerpieces: include sturdy options like small potted herbs, metal/glass lanterns with LED candles, and wooden boxes with glass bottles anchored in sand/gravel
centerpieces: provide weighted bases or museum putty to prevent tipping in breezes
centerpieces: group multiple small centerpieces on wooden slices or trays to create a heavier unit
centerpieces: avoid open flames outdoors; use battery-operated LED candles
centerpieces: test one full centerpiece on-site in natural light before mass setup

kid-activities-plan: low-cost 60-minute rotation plan for children's activities; stations include crafts, scavenger hunt, bubble art, lawn games, story corner, coloring; supervision guideline: 1 adult/older teen per 2–3 stations; safety and allergy considerations; supplies list.
